title: Manpages: podman network connect missing example with --mac-address option
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Hello!

I added example with `--mac-address` option for `podman network connect`

Fixes: #26374 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Thanks, you will have to add a DCO signoff to the commit message see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs

Also if you are going to repush it would be nice to add the `Fixes: #26374` also as part of the commit message.

Thanks for your check.
I added the DCO signoff and `Fixes: #26374` to the commit message and repush it.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *shu-kitamura* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@Luap99 May I ask a question?

I have read [the code review process](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). I understand that PRs are merged in the following way:

- In phase 1, the `lgtm` label is added based on the reviewer's comments
- In phase 2, the `approved` label is added based on the approver's comments
- In phase 3, it is automatically merged (if the conditions are met)

Currently, the `approved` label has been added, but the `lgtm` label has not yet been added.
Do I need to get another reviewer to review this PR?

> * In phase 1, the `lgtm` label is added based on the reviewer's comments
> * In phase 2, the `approved` label is added based on the approver's comments



> @Luap99 May I ask a question?
> 
> I have read [the code review process](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). I understand that PRs are merged in the following way:
> 
> * In phase 1, the `lgtm` label is added based on the reviewer's comments
> * In phase 2, the `approved` label is added based on the approver's comments
> * In phase 3, it is automatically merged (if the conditions are met)
> 
> Currently, the `approved` label has been added, but the `lgtm` label has not yet been added. Do I need to get another reviewer to review this PR?

Yes, the process requires a second lgtm where the `lgtm` is preceeded by a `/`.  Also, imho, phase 2 can occur before phase 1 because we don't really take advantage of the approved label.  I will review and merge your PR now if it is up to snuff.
